message.text:
Read carefully the project files, the database, and the datamodel. I would like to design the REST API of my project.

Design all REST endpoints. For each endpoint, provide the following in tabular format:
   - HTTP method
   - URL
   - Short description
   - Permission level (public / logged-in / admin / owner)
   - Relevant status codes (success + possible errors)
Group them by resource. Use nested resource URLs where justified, and query parameters for filtering.

response.value:
**Designing REST Endpoints**

I'm focusing on designing REST endpoints grouped by resource, such as Users, UserRoles, Events, and more like TicketTypes nested under events. I need to define permissions, status codes, and ensure the tables are clear and concise, probably using Markdown formatting. Each resource will have a header in **Title Case** and include fields like Method, URL, Description, Permission, and Status Codes. I want to present this neatly, making it easy to understand for the user.

**Structuring Nested Resource URLs**

I’m working on nested resource URLs, like using `/events/{event_id}/ticket-types` for ticket types under events. Ticket reservations could be under either `/events/{event_id}/reservations` or `/users/{user_id}/reservations`. I’ll also establish URLs for tickets, payments, refunds, and sales data. I need to ensure permission levels are clear, such as owner permissions for updates, "logged-in" for ticket reservations, and public for viewing events. It’s important to keep these tables concise yet complete and include relevant status codes. Next steps will be to provide a progress update and outline further actions.

**Finalizing API Design**

I’m getting ready to finalize the API design, which will include tables for various resources like Auth, Users, UserRoles, Events, and more, such as TicketTypes and Payments. I’ll incorporate nested resource URLs using placeholders like `{event_id}` for clarity. It’s essential to keep the tables concise. I need to ensure the formatting is correct with section headers in **Title Case** and bullets for keywords. I’ll prepare to present the final answer while sticking to these guidelines.
response.generatedTitle: Reviewed API design and considered resource URL structure

response.value:
Progress: I inspected the DB schema and entity docs and extracted entities/relations; below are grouped REST endpoints per resource.

**Auth & Session**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| POST | /auth/register | Register new user | public | 201, 400 (validation), 409 (email exists), 500 |
| POST | /auth/login | Authenticate user, return token/session | public | 200, 400, 401 (invalid creds), 500 |
| POST | /auth/logout | Invalidate current session/token | logged-in | 204, 401 |
| GET | /auth/me | Current user profile | logged-in | 200, 401 |

**Users**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /users | List users (with pagination & filters: ?email=&status=) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| POST | /users | Create user (admin-created) | admin | 201, 400, 409 |
| GET | /users/{user_id} | Get user profile | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PUT | /users/{user_id} | Update user (profile fields) | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /users/{user_id}/status | Change status (deactivate/delete) | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| DELETE | /users/{user_id} | Delete user (soft/hard per policy) | admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |

**UserRoles**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /users/{user_id}/roles | List roles for a user | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /users/{user_id}/roles | Assign role to user (body: role) | admin | 201, 400, 401, 403, 409 |
| DELETE | /users/{user_id}/roles/{user_role_id} | Remove role | admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |

**Events**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /events | List events with filters: ?start_from=&start_to=&location=&category=&min_price=&max_price=&published=&organizer_id=&q=&sort=&page=&limit= | public (only published visible) / logged-in (see published filter) | 200, 400 |
| POST | /events | Create event | logged-in | 201, 400, 401 |
| GET | /events/{event_id} | Get event details | public (if published & visibility rules) / owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PUT | /events/{event_id} | Replace event (full update) | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /events/{event_id} | Partial update | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| DELETE | /events/{event_id} | Delete event | owner / admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /events/{event_id}/publish | Publish or unpublish event (body: {published: true/false}) | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /events/{event_id}/attendees | List attendees / purchasers (for organizer/admin) | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |

Notes: public listing returns only published/visible events; organizers and admins can list unpublished events using ?organizer_id or special flag.

**Ticket Types (nested under Event)**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /events/{event_id}/ticket-types | List ticket types for event | public (if event published) / owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /events/{event_id}/ticket-types | Create ticket type for event | owner / admin | 201, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /events/{event_id}/ticket-types/{ticket_type_id} | Get ticket type | public / owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PUT | /events/{event_id}/ticket-types/{ticket_type_id} | Update ticket type | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| DELETE | /events/{event_id}/ticket-types/{ticket_type_id} | Delete ticket type | owner / admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |

Query /events endpoint supports min_price/max_price filtering derived from ticket_types.

**Ticket Reservations**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| POST | /events/{event_id}/reservations | Create reservation (body: user_id, ticket_type_id, seat?) | logged-in | 201, 400, 401, 403, 409 (seat taken), 404 |
| GET | /users/{user_id}/reservations | List user's reservations | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| GET | /events/{event_id}/reservations | List reservations for event (organizer/admin) with ?status= | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| GET | /reservations/{reservation_id} | Get reservation | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /reservations/{reservation_id} | Update reservation (e.g., change seat, cancel) | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /reservations/{reservation_id}/cancel | Initiate cancellation | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| DELETE | /reservations/{reservation_id} | Remove reservation (admin) | admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |

Notes: Reservation creation enforces unique active seat constraint; responses may include expires_at and payment link.

**Tickets**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /tickets | List tickets (admin filterable by event/user/status) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| GET | /users/{user_id}/tickets | List tickets for user | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| GET | /tickets/{ticket_id} | Get ticket details | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /reservations/{reservation_id}/tickets | Issue ticket for reservation (after payment) | system / admin | 201, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /tickets/{ticket_id} | Update ticket (status, seat) | owner / admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| DELETE | /tickets/{ticket_id} | Cancel ticket (admin/process) | admin | 204, 401, 403, 404 |

**Payments**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| POST | /reservations/{reservation_id}/payments | Create/attempt payment for a reservation | logged-in | 201, 400, 401, 402 (payment required/failure), 404, 409 |
| GET | /payments/{payment_id} | Get payment status/details | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /reservations/{reservation_id}/payments | List payments for reservation | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /payments/{payment_id}/confirm | Confirm async payment callback (webhook) | system (signed) | 200, 400, 401, 403 |
| POST | /payments/{payment_id}/capture | Capture payment (if separate) | admin / system | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |

Relevant error statuses include 402/409 for payment issues, 500 for gateway errors.

**Refunds**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| POST | /payments/{payment_id}/refunds | Initiate refund for a payment (body: amount, reason) | owner (if eligible) / admin | 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409 |
| GET | /refunds/{refund_id} | Get refund status | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /payments/{payment_id}/refunds | List refunds for payment | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /refunds/{refund_id} | Update refund (admin processing) | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |

**Sales Data / Reporting**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /events/{event_id}/sales | Get aggregated sales data for event | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /sales (with ?start=&end=&organizer_id=&export=csv) | Global sales report / export | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403 |
| POST | /events/{event_id}/sales/refresh | Trigger recompute/update sales metrics | admin / system | 202, 400, 401, 403, 404 |

**Admin & Management**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /admin/events/unpublished | List unpublished events (for review) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| POST | /admin/events/{event_id}/approve | Approve and activate event listing | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /admin/users/{user_id}/impersonate | Start impersonation session (admin) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |

**Search / Filters (Event listing query parameters)**
- ?q= (fulltext on title/description)
- ?start_from= & ?start_to= (timestamps)
- ?location=
- ?category=
- ?min_price= & ?max_price= (derived from ticket_types price)
- ?published= (true|false)
- ?organizer_id=
- ?sort= (start_time, price, popularity)
- ?page= & ?limit=

Permission notes and ownership rules
- public: unauthenticated access (only to published/visible events and public ticket type info)
- logged-in: authenticated users can create events, make reservations, payments, see their own resources
- owner: resource owner (e.g., event.organizer_id, reservation.user_id) can view/manage resource; owner endpoints must verify ownership
- admin: global administrators can manage users, roles, approve/publish events, export sales, and access all resources

Common status code guidance (used across endpoints)
- 200 OK — successful GET/PUT/PATCH (resource returned)
- 201 Created — resource created (POST)
- 202 Accepted — async action accepted (e.g., sales refresh)
- 204 No Content — successful deletion or logout
- 400 Bad Request — validation or malformed request
- 401 Unauthorized — missing/invalid auth
- 403 Forbidden — authenticated but insufficient permissions
- 404 Not Found — resource not found or not accessible
- 409 Conflict — business conflict (e.g., duplicate email, seat already reserved)
- 402 Payment Required / domain-specific payment-failure codes
- 500 Internal Server Error — server or gateway errors

If you want, I can:
- expand each table row with example request/response JSON schemas, or
- generate an OpenAPI (YAML/JSON) spec from this design. Which next step do you prefer?

<context>
The current date is March 14, 2026.
</context>
<reminderInstructions>
You are an agent—keep going until the user's query is completely resolved before ending your turn. ONLY stop if solved or genuinely blocked.
Take action when possible; the user expects you to do useful work without unnecessary questions.
After any parallel, read-only context gathering, give a concise progress update and what's next.
Avoid repetition across turns: don't restate unchanged plans or sections (like the todo list) verbatim; provide delta updates or only the parts that changed.
Tool batches: You MUST preface each batch with a one-sentence why/what/outcome preamble.
Progress cadence: After 3 to 5 tool calls, or when you create/edit > ~3 files in a burst, report progress.
Requirements coverage: Read the user's ask in full and think carefully. Do not omit a requirement. If something cannot be done with available tools, note why briefly and propose a viable alternative.
Skip filler acknowledgements like "Sounds good" or "Okay, I will…". Open with a purposeful one-liner about what you're doing next.
When sharing setup or run steps, present terminal commands in fenced code blocks with the correct language tag. Keep commands copyable and on separate lines.
Avoid definitive claims about the build or runtime setup unless verified from the provided context (or quick tool checks). If uncertain, state what's known from attachments and proceed with minimal steps you can adapt later.
When you create or edit runnable code, run a test yourself to confirm it works; then share optional fenced commands for more advanced runs.
For non-trivial code generation, produce a complete, runnable solution: necessary source files, a tiny runner or test/benchmark harness, a minimal `README.md`, and updated dependency manifests (e.g., `package.json`, `requirements.txt`, `pyproject.toml`). Offer quick "try it" commands and optional platform-specific speed-ups when relevant.
Your goal is to act like a pair programmer: be friendly and helpful. If you can do more, do more. Be proactive with your solutions, think about what the user needs and what they want, and implement it proactively.
<importantReminders>
Do NOT volunteer your model name unless the user explicitly asks you about it. 
You MUST use the todo list tool to plan and track your progress. NEVER skip this step, and START with this step whenever the task is multi-step. This is essential for maintaining visibility and proper execution of large tasks.
When referring to a filename or symbol in the user's workspace, wrap it in backticks.

</importantReminders>

</reminderInstructions>

   - HTTP method
   - URL
   - Short description
   - Permission level (public / logged-in / admin / owner)
   - Relevant status codes (success + possible errors)
Group them by resource. Use nested resource URLs where justified, and query parameters for filtering.
</userRequest>

| POST | /payments/{payment_id}/refunds | Initiate refund for a payment (body: amount, reason) | owner (if eligible) / admin | 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409 |
| GET | /refunds/{refund_id} | Get refund status | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /payments/{payment_id}/refunds | List refunds for payment | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| PATCH | /refunds/{refund_id} | Update refund (admin processing) | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |

**Sales Data / Reporting**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /events/{event_id}/sales | Get aggregated sales data for event | owner / admin | 200, 401, 403, 404 |
| GET | /sales (with ?start=&end=&organizer_id=&export=csv) | Global sales report / export | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403 |
| POST | /events/{event_id}/sales/refresh | Trigger recompute/update sales metrics | admin / system | 202, 400, 401, 403, 404 |

**Admin & Management**
| Method | URL | Short description | Permission | Relevant status codes |
|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| GET | /admin/events/unpublished | List unpublished events (for review) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |
| POST | /admin/events/{event_id}/approve | Approve and activate event listing | admin | 200, 400, 401, 403, 404 |
| POST | /admin/users/{user_id}/impersonate | Start impersonation session (admin) | admin | 200, 401, 403 |

**Search / Filters (Event listing query parameters)**
- ?q= (fulltext on title/description)
- ?start_from= & ?start_to= (timestamps)
- ?location=
- ?category=
- ?min_price= & ?max_price= (derived from ticket_types price)
- ?published= (true|false)
- ?organizer_id=
- ?sort= (start_time, price, popularity)
- ?page= & ?limit=

Permission notes and ownership rules
- public: unauthenticated access (only to published/visible events and public ticket type info)
- logged-in: authenticated users can create events, make reservations, payments, see their own resources
- owner: resource owner (e.g., event.organizer_id, reservation.user_id) can view/manage resource; owner endpoints must verify ownership
- admin: global administrators can manage users, roles, approve/publish events, export sales, and access all resources

Common status code guidance (used across endpoints)
- 200 OK — successful GET/PUT/PATCH (resource returned)
- 201 Created — resource created (POST)
- 202 Accepted — async action accepted (e.g., sales refresh)
- 204 No Content — successful deletion or logout
- 400 Bad Request — validation or malformed request
- 401 Unauthorized — missing/invalid auth
- 403 Forbidden — authenticated but insufficient permissions
- 404 Not Found — resource not found or not accessible
- 409 Conflict — business conflict (e.g., duplicate email, seat already reserved)
- 402 Payment Required / domain-specific payment-failure codes
- 500 Internal Server Error — server or gateway errors

If you want, I can:
- expand each table row with example request/response JSON schemas, or
- generate an OpenAPI (YAML/JSON) spec from this design. Which next step do you prefer?

children.text: -- PostgreSQL SQL file defining all tables
user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('active', 'deactivated', 'deleted')),
event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
visibility BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
published BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
ticket_type_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
event_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES events(event_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Ticket types cannot exist without their event
reservation_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
event_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES events(event_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Reservations are tied to events
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Reservations are tied to users
ticket_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES ticket_types(ticket_type_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Reservations are tied to ticket types
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('active', 'expired', 'cancelled')),
ticket_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
ticket_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES ticket_types(ticket_type_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Tickets cannot exist without their ticket type
reservation_id UUID REFERENCES ticket_reservations(reservation_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
-- ON DELETE SET NULL: Tickets may exist independently after reservation expires or is cancelled
user_id UUID REFERENCES users(user_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
-- ON DELETE SET NULL: Tickets may remain for reporting after user deletion
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('issued', 'reserved', 'cancelled')),
payment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
reservation_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES ticket_reservations(reservation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Payments are tied to reservations
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'completed', 'failed')),
refund_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
payment_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES payments(payment_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Refunds are tied to payments
reservation_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES ticket_reservations(reservation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Refunds are tied to reservations
status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'processed', 'failed')),
requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
sales_data_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
event_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES events(event_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Sales data is tied to events
total_tickets_sold INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (total_tickets_sold >= 0),
total_revenue NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (total_revenue >= 0),
user_role_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
-- ON DELETE CASCADE: Roles are tied to users
-- Indexes for search/filter criteria (from feature card Input fields)
-- Unique seat reservation for active status already enforced by unique_seat_event_active
-- Unique user email already enforced by UNIQUE constraint
-- Application-level invariants: Only published events are displayed, filtering, sorting, display logic, event visibility after review, activation after validation
-- Database-level invariant: Event start_time must not be in the past
ALTER TABLE events ADD CONSTRAINT check_event_start_time_future CHECK (start_time >= NOW());
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX unique_seat_event_active ON ticket_reservations (event_id, seat) WHERE status = 'active';
-- Application-level invariants: Reservation expiry, cancellation eligibility, refund reliability, prompt cancellation, notification
-- Database-level invariant: Pricing must be valid and non-negative
-- Database-level invariant: User data must be unique
-- Already enforced by email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE
-- Application-level invariants: Role assignment/update/removal must be valid and authorized, displayed roles must be accurate
-- Filtering, sorting, and display rules (event list, dashboard, export)
-- Event visibility and activation after review

INSERT INTO user_roles (user_role_id, user_id, role, assigned_at, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO user_roles (user_role_id, user_id, role, assigned_at, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO events (event_id, organizer_id, title, description, category, location, start_time, end_time, visibility, published, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

SELECT gen_random_uuid(), user_id, 'Tech Meetup', 'Networking for developers', 'technology', 'Debrecen', NOW() + INTERVAL '14 days', NOW() + INTERVAL '14 days' + INTERVAL '3 hours', TRUE, FALSE, '{}', NOW(), NOW() FROM users WHERE email = 'alice@example.com';
INSERT INTO ticket_types (ticket_type_id, event_id, name, price, quantity, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO ticket_types (ticket_type_id, event_id, name, price, quantity, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO ticket_types (ticket_type_id, event_id, name, price, quantity, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO ticket_reservations (reservation_id, event_id, user_id, ticket_type_id, seat, status, expires_at, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

INSERT INTO sales_data (sales_data_id, event_id, total_tickets_sold, total_revenue, last_updated, metadata, created_at, updated_at)

This document lists the candidate database tables for the EventHub application, extracted from feature cards. Each entity is owned by the feature(s) that create, update, or delete it.

- Owned by: Select Seat, Select Ticket Type, Reserve Ticket, Initiate Booking Cancellation, Validate Cancellation Eligibility, Process Cancellation Request, Confirm Cancellation
- Owned by: Enter Payment Information, Validate Payment, Handle Payment Failure
- Owned by: Initiate Refund Request, Validate Refund Eligibility, Process Refund Payment, Notify Refund Status
- Referenced by: Display Sales Dashboard, Export Sales Data
- Owned by: Create User Account, Update User Information, Deactivate User Account, Delete User Account
- Owned by: Assign User Role, Update User Role, Remove User Role
This list forms the basis for the PostgreSQL schema. Each entity will be defined as a table, with relationships and fields specified in the next step.

edits.text:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: EventHub API
  description: REST API for EventHub (events, ticketing, payments, refunds, users, sales)
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000/api
paths:
  /auth/register:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Register a new user
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuthRegister'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: User created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '409':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict'

  /auth/login:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Authenticate a user and return JWT
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuthLogin'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Authentication successful
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuthResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'

  /auth/logout:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Logout current user (invalidate token)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Logged out
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'

  /auth/me:
    get:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Get current authenticated user profile
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Current user
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'

  /users:
    get:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: List users (admin only)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: email
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: status
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: page
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
        - in: query
          name: limit
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of users
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
    post:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Create user (admin)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserCreate'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: User created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '409':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict'

  /users/{user_id}:
    parameters:
      - in: path
        name: user_id
        required: true
        schema:
          type: string
          format: uuid
    get:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Get user by id
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: User object
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'
    put:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Replace user
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserUpdate'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated user
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'
    delete:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Delete user (admin)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '204':
          description: User deleted
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'

  /users/{user_id}/roles:
    parameters:
      - in: path
        name: user_id
        required: true
        schema:
          type: string
          format: uuid
    get:
      tags: [UserRoles]
      summary: List roles for a user
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Roles list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserRoleResponse'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
    post:
      tags: [UserRoles]
      summary: Assign role to user
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserRoleCreate'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Role assigned
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserRoleResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '409':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict'

  /users/{user_id}/roles/{user_role_id}:
    parameters:
      - in: path
        name: user_id
        required: true
        schema:
          type: string
          format: uuid
      - in: path
        name: user_role_id
        required: true
        schema:
          type: string
          format: uuid
    delete:
      tags: [UserRoles]
      summary: Remove role from user
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Role removed
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'

  /events:
    get:
      tags: [Events]
      summary: List events with filters
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: q
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: start_from
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date-time
        - in: query
          name: start_to
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date-time
        - in: query
          name: location
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: category
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: min_price
          schema:
            type: number
        - in: query
          name: max_price
          schema:
            type: number
        - in: query
          name: published
          schema:
            type: boolean
        - in: query
          name: organizer_id
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
        - in: query
          name: sort
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: page
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
        - in: query
          name: limit
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of events
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/EventResponse'
        '400':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest'
    post:
      tags: [Events]
      summary: Create a new event
      security:

    put:

        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'
    patch:
      tags: [Events]

      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true

          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/EventUpdate'

        '200':

        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':

        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'

  /reservations/{reservation_id}:

  /reservations/{reservation_id}/payments:
    post:
      tags: [Payments]
      summary: Create/attempt payment for a reservation
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:

  /payments/{payment_id}:

  /events/{event_id}/sales:

components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
  responses:
    BadRequest:
      description: Bad request
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    Unauthorized:
      description: Unauthorized
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    Forbidden:
      description: Forbidden
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    NotFound:
      description: Not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    Conflict:
      description: Conflict
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
  schemas:
    ErrorResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        code:
          type: integer
          example: 400
        message:
          type: string
          example: Validation failed

    AuthRegister:
      type: object
      required: [email, password]
      properties:
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
          example: newuser@example.com
        password:
          type: string
          example: strongP@ssw0rd
        full_name:
          type: string
          example: New User

    AuthLogin:
      type: object
      required: [email, password]
      properties:
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
          example: bob@example.com
        password:
          type: string
          example: hash2

    AuthResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        token:
          type: string
          example: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
        expires_in:
          type: integer
          example: 3600
        user:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/UserResponse'

    UserCreate:
      type: object
      required: [email, password]
      properties:
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
          example: alice@example.com
        password:
          type: string
          example: hash1
        full_name:
          type: string
          example: Alice Smith

    UserUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        full_name:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, deactivated, deleted]

    UserResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        user_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          example: 3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6
        email:
          type: string
          example: alice@example.com
        full_name:
          type: string
          example: Alice Smith
        status:
          type: string
          example: active
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          example: 2026-03-01T12:00:00Z

    UserRoleCreate:
      type: object
      required: [role]
      properties:
        role:
          type: string
          example: organizer

    UserRoleResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        user_role_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          example: 7c9e6679-7425-40de-944b-e07fc1f90ae7
        user_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        role:
          type: string
          example: organizer
        assigned_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    EventCreate:
      type: object
      required: [title, location, start_time, end_time]
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
          example: Spring Concert
        description:
          type: string
          example: Live music event
        category:
          type: string
          example: music
        location:
          type: string
          example: Budapest
        start_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          example: 2026-03-21T19:00:00Z
        end_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          example: 2026-03-21T21:00:00Z
        visibility:
          type: boolean
          example: true
        published:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        metadata:
          type: object

    EventUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        category:
          type: string
        location:
          type: string
        start_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        end_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        visibility:
          type: boolean
        published:
          type: boolean
        metadata:
          type: object

    EventResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        event_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        organizer_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        title:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        category:
          type: string
        location:
          type: string
        start_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        end_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        visibility:
          type: boolean
        published:
          type: boolean
        metadata:
          type: object
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    TicketTypeCreate:
      type: object
      required: [name, price, quantity]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
          example: VIP
        price:
          type: number
          format: decimal
          example: 15000
        quantity:
          type: integer
          example: 10
        metadata:
          type: object

    TicketTypeUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        price:
          type: number
          format: decimal
        quantity:
          type: integer
        metadata:
          type: object

    TicketTypeResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        ticket_type_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        event_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        name:
          type: string
        price:
          type: number
        quantity:
          type: integer
        metadata:
          type: object

    ReservationCreate:
      type: object
      required: [ticket_type_id]
      properties:
        user_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        ticket_type_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        seat:
          type: string
          example: A1

    ReservationUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        seat:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, expired, cancelled]

    ReservationResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        reservation_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        event_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        user_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        ticket_type_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        seat:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
        expires_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    TicketResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        ticket_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        ticket_type_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        reservation_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        user_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        seat:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
        issued_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    PaymentCreate:
      type: object
      required: [amount]
      properties:
        amount:
          type: number
          format: decimal
        method:
          type: string
          example: card
        gateway_data:
          type: object

    PaymentResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        payment_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        reservation_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        amount:
          type: number
        status:
          type: string
        method:
          type: string
        processed_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    RefundCreate:
      type: object
      required: [amount, reservation_id]
      properties:
        reservation_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        amount:
          type: number
        reason:
          type: string

    RefundResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        refund_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        payment_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        reservation_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        amount:
          type: number
        status:
          type: string
        requested_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    SalesDataResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        sales_data_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        event_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        total_tickets_sold:
          type: integer
          example: 42
        total_revenue:
          type: number
          example: 420000.00
        last_updated:
          type: string
          format: date-time

response.value: . Would you like an OpenAPI JSON export or an example Postman collection next?

agent.description: Edit files in your workspace in agent mode
agent.name: agent

renderedUserMessage.text:
<context>
The current date is March 14, 2026.
<todoList>
# Todo List

- [x] Explore repository files and DB schema
- [x] Extract data model (entities & relations)
- [-] Design REST endpoints per resource
- [ ] Assemble endpoints into grouped tables
- [ ] Review and deliver API design
</todoList>

</context>
<reminderInstructions>
You are an agent—keep going until the user's query is completely resolved before ending your turn. ONLY stop if solved or genuinely blocked.
Take action when possible; the user expects you to do useful work without unnecessary questions.
After any parallel, read-only context gathering, give a concise progress update and what's next.
Avoid repetition across turns: don't restate unchanged plans or sections (like the todo list) verbatim; provide delta updates or only the parts that changed.
Tool batches: You MUST preface each batch with a one-sentence why/what/outcome preamble.
Progress cadence: After 3 to 5 tool calls, or when you create/edit > ~3 files in a burst, report progress.
Requirements coverage: Read the user's ask in full and think carefully. Do not omit a requirement. If something cannot be done with available tools, note why briefly and propose a viable alternative.
Skip filler acknowledgements like "Sounds good" or "Okay, I will…". Open with a purposeful one-liner about what you're doing next.
When sharing setup or run steps, present terminal commands in fenced code blocks with the correct language tag. Keep commands copyable and on separate lines.
Avoid definitive claims about the build or runtime setup unless verified from the provided context (or quick tool checks). If uncertain, state what's known from attachments and proceed with minimal steps you can adapt later.
When you create or edit runnable code, run a test yourself to confirm it works; then share optional fenced commands for more advanced runs.

openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: EventHub API
  description: REST API for EventHub (events, ticketing, payments, refunds, users, sales)
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000/api
paths:

    post:

        '201':
          description: User created

        '400':
